title: Tithymalus platyphyllos
taxonomy: Tithymalus platyphyllos is classified within the family Euphorbiaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Tithymalus, which is a part of the larger family. The species is native to Europe and Western Asia, and it is known for its medicinal and ornamental properties. Tithymalus platyphyllos is a type of spurge, a common name for plants within the Euphorbiaceae family. It is often found in dry areas and rocky terrain, where it can thrive in poor soil conditions.
uses: Tithymalus platyphyllos, also known as the broad-leaved spontaneous dodder, has been traditionally used in folk medicine.
